message:
feat(tmux): add PasteKeys with bracketed paste mode for reliable multiline input

Introduces a new PasteKeys() method that wraps text in bracketed paste
escape sequences (\e[200~ ... \e[201~) before sending to tmux panes.
This prevents shells and applications from interpreting special
characters or prematurely executing multiline text.

The bracketed paste protocol is supported by most modern terminals and
readline-based applications. It signals to the receiving application
that the content is being pasted rather than typed, allowing it to
handle the input atomically.

Changes:
- Add PasteKeys() method to tmux.Client with chunked sending support
- Add PasteKeys() convenience function for default client
- Update pane name regex to allow dashes in plugin agent types
- Switch all prompt-sending code paths to use PasteKeys:
  * cli/add.go: CASS context and user prompt injection
  * cli/replay.go: replaying prompts from history
  * cli/send.go: broadcasting prompts to agent panes
  * cli/spawn.go: initial prompt and context injection
  * cli/watch.go: file-triggered command execution
  * palette/model.go: sending selected palette commands
  * auth/claude.go: continuation prompts during auth flow

This is particularly important for:
- Multiline prompts with code blocks or special characters
- Prompts containing shell metacharacters (|, &, ;, etc.)
- Large prompts that might otherwise be split across multiple lines

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
